hq: 264 Queens Quay West, Suite 328, Toronto ON M5J 1B5, Canada

system_description.overview: Regy (branded as LienFluent) is a legal registry search platform that automates lien and registry searches across Canadian and US jurisdictions. It provides instant search results, AI-generated summaries, and a search API for legal and financial professionals.

products.Registry Searches: Automated searches across multiple registries (PPSA, UCC, corporate registries)
products.Search Summaries and Exports: AI-generated summaries and exportable search results
products.Registry Search API: Programmatic access to registry search functionality for integration with third-party systems

infrastructure.physical_presence: None (no physical office; registered address is a virtual office)

network_architecture.network_segmentation: Segmented VPCs with network ACLs; production environment isolated from development and test
network_architecture.firewalls: Application gateway firewall and production network firewall; firewall rules restrict unauthorized access
network_architecture.ids_ips: IDS/IPS provided via AWS infrastructure controls
network_architecture.vpn: Bastion host used for secure administrative access
network_architecture.tls: TLS in transit for all communications
network_architecture.topology: Internet → AWS VPC (segmented) → Regy Application → AWS Database; external search registries and Summary Generator accessed via API; bastion host for admin access; AWS manages underlying physical infrastructure (carve-out)

application_architecture.pattern: Not explicitly disclosed; cloud-native on AWS
compute: AWS EC2 or equivalent (not explicitly named)
application_architecture.api_style: REST (Registry Search API offered to customers)
application_architecture.environments: Separate development, test, and production environments

databases: AWS RDS or equivalent (not explicitly named)

authentication_access_control.mfa: MFA enforced for source code access and administrative accounts
authentication_access_control.password_policy: Password complexity and expiry enforced via AWS IAM policies

ci_cd_devops.source_control: Git-based with MFA (specific platform not named)
ci_cd_devops.branch_protection: Merge approval required before merging to main branch; branch protection rules enforced
ci_cd_devops.patch_management: Automatic patch management; CTO responsible for overseeing patch process

third_party_services.purpose: External registry data sources (PPSA, UCC, corporate registries)

security_tools.antivirus: Antivirus deployed on endpoints; weekly definition updates

excluded_criteria: CC6.4 - Physical access controls delegated to AWS (carve-out)
A1.2 - No physical office; physical environmental controls not applicable
notable_controls: Segmented VPCs with network ACLs for production isolation
1 business day access revocation SLA — explicit and enforceable
Annual BC/DR testing covering multiple loss scenarios
Branch protection with required merge approvals
Formal change management process with emergency change procedure
Registry Search API offered externally — additional attack surface managed via separate network controls

infrastructure.rationale: Multi-AZ on AWS with segmented VPCs and network ACLs. No multi-region, no CDN mentioned, IaC not confirmed. Standard AWS carve-out deployment.

application_architecture.rationale: Limited disclosure. Separate environments and branch protection confirmed. Registry Search API is a notable product capability. No language, framework, or architecture pattern disclosed.

data_layer.rationale: AWS-based database (specific service not named). Data classification and retention policies in place. Multi-AZ. No caching, no data warehouse, backup frequency not stated.

security_posture.rationale: Monthly vuln scans + annual pen test + antivirus + IDS/IPS + server hardening + MFA. Application gateway and network firewalls in place. No WAF explicitly mentioned. No SIEM named.

devops_maturity.rationale: Branch protection, separate environments, formal change management, and patch management confirmed. No CI/CD tool named, IaC not confirmed, no container orchestration disclosed.

bcdr_readiness.rationale: BCDR policy with annual review and testing across multiple scenarios, multi-AZ failover. RTO/RPO not disclosed. No multi-region failover. Daily backups implied.

vendor_diversity.rationale: Primary dependency on AWS plus external registry APIs and a summary generator. Core business depends on third-party registry data availability. Manageable but registry API uptime is outside Regy's control.

overall.rationale: Solid foundational SOC 2 posture with full five trust criteria scope — impressive for a Type 1. Multi-AZ, quarterly access reviews, annual pen test, segmented VPCs, and 1-day revocation SLA are positives. Significant gaps in technical disclosure (no named tech stack) and heavy dependency on external registry APIs that are outside their control.

red_flags: Core product functionality (registry searches) depends on third-party external APIs outside Regy's control
No WAF explicitly mentioned despite external-facing API product
Database and storage services not named — cannot assess specific AWS service configuration
yellow_flags: Limited application architecture disclosure — no languages, frameworks, or architecture pattern named
SOC 2 Type 1 only — no operational effectiveness demonstrated over time
Company legal name (13139026 Canada Inc.) is a numbered company — minimal public corporate presence
green_flags: Full five-criteria SOC 2 scope (Security, Availability, Processing Integrity, Confidentiality, Privacy) — unusually broad for a Type 1
Explicit 1 business day access revocation SLA
Segmented VPCs with network ACLs for production isolation
Branch protection with required merge approvals
Annual BC/DR testing covering multiple loss scenarios
Formal emergency change procedure in place
key_observations: The 5-criteria SOC 2 Type 1 scope is notable — most early-stage companies only cover Security. This signals intentional compliance investment.
Business model critically depends on external registry API availability — PPSA, UCC, and corporate registry downtime would directly break the product
Registry Search API as an external product offering expands the attack surface beyond typical SaaS; network segmentation appears designed to address this
The company legal entity is a numbered Canadian corporation — Regy/LienFluent may be in an early rebranding phase (website is lienfluent.com)
Signed personally by CEO Michael Cain — indicates founder-level accountability for security posture, common for early-stage companies
